steps.title: Set up the weight formula
steps.content:
The calf weighs 5,400 pounds at birth and gains 250 pounds per day. After $n$ days, the calf's weight is:
$$W = 5,400 + 250n$$

steps.title: Translate the constraint into an inequality
steps.content:
The weight must be greater than 8,900 and less than 9,900 pounds. This gives the compound inequality:
$$8,900 \lt 5,400 + 250n \lt 9,900$$

steps.title: Solve the inequality
steps.content:
Subtract 5,400 from all parts:
$$3,500 \lt 250n \lt 4,500$$
Divide all parts by 250:
$$14 \lt n \lt 18$$
Since $n$ must be an integer and $14 \lt n \lt 18$, the possible values are 15, 16, and 17.
